task_completion.description: Verify a local Docker registry is started on port 5000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'docker run -d -p 5000:5000 registry' or 'docker ps' displaying a registry container with port 5000 mapped.

task_completion.description: Verify a new Docker Buildx builder instance is created using the 'docker-container' driver.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker buildx create --driver docker-container' (possibly with network flags) or 'docker buildx ls' listing a builder with the docker-container driver.

task_completion.description: Verify the new Buildx builder is bootstrapped and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker buildx inspect --bootstrap' or 'docker buildx use' followed by output confirming the builder endpoint is running/active.

task_completion.description: Verify the Go application is built for both 'linux/amd64' and 'linux/arm64' platforms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a 'docker buildx build' command with the flag '--platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64' and standard Docker build output (e.g., resolving layers, building steps).

task_completion.description: Verify the resulting multi-arch image is pushed to 'localhost:5000/edge-app:latest'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the '--push' flag in the build command and output indicating a successful push to 'localhost:5000/edge-app:latest' (e.g., '=> pushing manifest for localhost:5000/edge-app:latest').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used actual terminal commands to configure Docker and build the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard shell prompts, typed Docker commands, and authentic Docker daemon/buildx output rather than a text editor mocking the output.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just echo or print success messages to fake the build.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the output contains standard Docker build logs (e.g., '=> [internal] load build definition', '=> exporting to image') and not just a simple printed string.

integrity.description: Verify the agent built the application from the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The build command should reference '/home/ga/edge-app/' or the terminal prompt should show the agent is inside '~/edge-app' or '/home/ga/edge-app' during the build.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application directory is /home/ga/edge-app. The Docker registry must run on port 5000. The target image is 'localhost:5000/edge-app:latest'. The target platforms are 'linux/amd64' and 'linux/arm64'. A successful multi-arch push will result in a manifest list with media type 'application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json' or 'application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json'.
